original_abstract: This paper provides a detailed analysis of cross-border cooperation networks within the framework of the Poland-Slovakia Cross-Border Cooperation Programme 2007–2013. Utilizing social network analysis (SNA), the study characterizes the structural properties of these partnerships by examining metrics such as network density, centrality, cohesion, and the positioning of specific intersection points between bridging and hub entities. The central objective is to determine the extent of integration between public and non-public institutions, as well as between urban centers, within this transnational space. Furthermore, the evaluation explores whether these cooperative structures lean toward a centralized model or function as decentralized micro-networks. By identifying key stakeholders and the strength of their organizational ties, the research offers insights into the effectiveness and configuration of collaborative regional development across the Polish-Slovak border.
humanized_abstract: The paper presents analysis of cross-border cooperation networks within the Poland-Slovakia Cross-Border Cooperation Programme 2007–2013. The analysis was made by using social network analysis (SNA) methods, which are used to present the characteristics of cooperation networks, such as network density, centrality, cohesion, locations of border crossing points at the bridges and hubs. The main aim of the paper is to assess the degree of integration of the public and non-public sector entities and also the degree of integration of cities in the cross-border space, as well as to verify whether the cooperative networks are organized in a central way or rather as a collection of small micro-networks. In order to achieve the aim of the paper, we identified key actors in cross-border cooperation and the strength of relations between them, which in consequence enabled the assessment of their influence on the creation of a collaborative regional development space at the Polish-Slovak border.
